title: Rubus echinophorus

intro: Rubus echinophorus, also known as the Himalayan raspberry, is a species of bramble that belongs to the Rosaceae family of flowering plants. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help protect against oxidative stress, alleviate symptoms of various diseases, and support immune function. The bioactive constituents of Rubus echinophorus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and ascorbic acid, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties.
